hero.question: UFC Fight Night: Allan Nascimento vs. Mitch Raposo (Flyweight, Prelims)
hero.prob_take: A 50.0% split with $79K volume means the market has genuinely no read on this prelim matchup, and the 13.5 point drop signals recent action swinging toward the underdog rather than conviction either way. Nascimento's odds cratered on limited new information, which typically means sharp money is hunting value on Raposo at even money rather than the crowd having real intel on a skill mismatch.

daily_take.headline: Nascimento-Raposo Plunge Signals Sharp Money Fleeing Coin Flip
daily_take.deck: Nascimento dropped 13.5 points in 24 hours on minimal volume, now sitting at 50.0%. This isn't random drift. The collapse suggests informed bettors know something about fight dynamics, fighter conditioning, or late line movement that public markets haven't priced in yet. A preliminary flyweight bout doesn't move 13 points without reason. Short the favorites until the smart money shows its hand.
